tldr: Mathieu van der Poel doubled up with a second stage win — a reduced-group sprint after the day's Apennine climbing thinned the bunch. Pellizzari, Johannessen, Champoussin and Wout van Aert filled the top five same-time.

narrative.headline: Van der Poel's double, Van Aert top-5
narrative.body: 213 km, ~2,700 m of elevation, two long Apennine climbs (Ovindoli, Valico delle Capannelle) trimming the bunch. Reduced-group sprint into Martinsicuro; Van der Poel won, Van Aert's fifth was his strongest pre-Classics-spring result.

gcImpact.headline: Del Toro holds with time bonuses tightening
gcImpact.body: GC unchanged on time, bonuses tightened around the leader. Pellizzari now a clear secondary threat.

riderPerformances.name: Wout van Aert
riderPerformances.narrative: Fifth in the reduced-group sprint — solid sharpness check before the cobbled Classics block. Visma's Tirreno team built around him; the run to Roubaix would prove him in form.

riderPerformances.name: Mathieu van der Poel
riderPerformances.narrative: Second stage win, second reduced-group sprint after a climbing-and-positioning selection.

sources.title: Van der Poel double at Tirreno Stage 4
